results.title: 4D Primitive-Mâché: Glueing Primitives for Persistent 4D Scene Reconstruction
results.content:
In Tab. 1, we report per-sequence F-scores for all methods, alongside average F-score, recall, and precision. While (π3\pi^{3} last frame) achieves higher precision, its recall (i.e., scene coverage) is poor because information from all keyframes except the last is discarded. Other baselines perform well in terms of recall, indicating decent object coverage. However, most fall short in precision. Our method provides the best balance between precision and recall, achieving the highest F-score across all compared methods. [...] Our method decomposes the scene into a set of rigid 3D primitives, which are assumed to be moving throughout the scene. Using estimated dense 2D correspondences, we jointly infer the rigid motion of these primitives through an optimisation pipeline, yielding a 4D reconstruction of the scene, i.e. providing 3D geometry dynamically moving through time. To achieve this, we also introduce a mechanism to extrapolate motion for objects that become invisible, employing motion-grouping techniques to maintain continuity. [...] ## 4 Limitations

Our system assumes that each primitive is rigid and thus cannot represent more intricate non-rigid deformations.
Extending the method to handle such deformations whilst maintaining computational efficiency remains an important direction for future work. Additionally, incremental mapping capabilities, where the scene representation is built and updated over extended sequences, have yet to be explored.

## 5 Conclusion

results.title: CVPR Poster 4D Primitive-Mâché: Glueing Primitives for Persistent 4D Scene Reconstruction
results.content:
a mechanism to extrapolate motion for objects that become invisible, employing motion-grouping techniques to maintain continuity.The resulting system enables 4D spatio-temporal awareness, offering capabilities such as replayable 3D reconstructions of articulated objects through time, multi-object scanning, and object permanence. On object scanning and multi-object datasets, our system significantly outperforms existing methods both quantitatively and qualitatively. [...] We present a dynamic reconstruction system that receives a casual monocular RGB video as input, and outputs a complete and persistent reconstruction of the scene. In other words, we reconstruct not only the the currently visible parts of the scene, but also all previously viewed parts, which enables replaying the complete reconstruction across all timesteps.Our method decomposes the scene into a set of rigid 3D primitives, which are assumed to be moving throughout the scene. Using estimated dense 2D correspondences, we jointly infer the rigid motion of these primitives through an optimisation pipeline, yielding a 4D reconstruction of the scene, i.e. providing 3D geometry dynamically moving through time. results.title: 4DPM
results.content:
Our method decomposes the scene into a set of rigid 3D primitives, which are assumed to be moving throughout the scene. Using estimated dense 2D correspondences, we jointly infer the rigid motion of these primitives through an optimisation pipeline, yielding a 4D reconstruction of the scene, i.e. providing 3D geometry dynamically moving through time. To achieve this, we also introduce a mechanism to extrapolate motion for objects that become invisible, employing motion-grouping techniques to maintain continuity. [...] ### Method

  Method Diagram 

An overview diagram of our method is shown above. Our system takes a monocular RGB video as input and reconstructs a persistent 4D representation of the scene by decomposing it into a set of rigidly moving 3D primitives.

 

### Citation

```
@inproceedings{Mazur:etal:CVPR2026 title={4D Primitive-Mache: Glueing Primitives for Persistent 4D Scene Reconstruction}, author={Kirill Mazur and Marwan Taher and Andrew Davison}, booktitle={IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (CVPR)}, year={2026}, } 
``` [...] # 4D Primitive-Mché

### Glueing Primitives for Persistent 4D Scene Reconstruction

### CVPR 2026 Oral

Kirill Mazur Marwan Taher Andrew Davison

Dyson Robotics Lab | Imperial College London

Paper

Code

Video

BibTeX

#### 4D Primitive-Mché builds persistent and replayable 4D reconstructions of dynamic scenes by glueing 3D primitives together.

### Demos

#### Object Permanence

 Our system exhibits object permanence, retaining knowledge of objects even when they cannot be seen (e.g. a milk carton in a closed fridge).

### Abstract

results.title: 📢📢📢𝐌𝐞𝐬𝐡𝐑𝐢𝐩𝐩𝐥𝐞: 𝐒𝐭𝐫𝐮𝐜𝐭𝐮𝐫𝐞𝐝 𝐀𝐮𝐭𝐨𝐫𝐞𝐠𝐫𝐞𝐬𝐬𝐢𝐯𝐞 𝐆𝐞𝐧𝐞𝐫𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧 𝐨𝐟 𝐀𝐫𝐭𝐢𝐬𝐭-𝐌𝐞𝐬𝐡𝐞𝐬 High-fidelity, topologically complete 3D assets that expand naturally… | Matthias Niessner
results.content:
mechanism that groups object motion and predicts future states, preserving object permanence in the reconstruction. Empirical evaluations on object scanning and multi-object datasets demonstrate that 4D Primitive-Mché significantly outperforms existing methods, both quantitatively and qualitatively. The reconstructed results enable capabilities like replayable 3D reconstructions through time, enhanced multi-object scanning, and robust handling of articulated, moving scenes from everyday monocular video input. By fusing rigid primitives with temporal optimization and motion continuity, the system advances the state of the art in persistent 4D scene understanding from casual video, with potential applications in robotics, augmented reality, and dynamic scene modeling. [...] The paper introduces 4D Primitive-Mché, a novel dynamic scene reconstruction system that processes a casual monocular RGB video to output a complete and persistent 4D reconstruction—meaning it reconstructs not just the currently visible scene but also all previously seen parts across time. The core idea is to decompose the environment into a set of rigid 3D primitives whose motion is jointly inferred using dense 2D correspondences from the video. This optimization captures both geometry and motion, enabling the system to produce a spatio-temporal model of the scene that tracks objects through time, handles occlusions, and maintains continuity even as objects become temporarily invisible. A key contribution is a motion extrapolation mechanism that groups object motion and predicts future [...] 4D Primitive-Mché: Glueing Primitives for Persistent 4D Scene Reconstruction   arxiv.org
